<!-- begin acp-response-tiers (managed by /setup:onboarding and /setup:update - do not edit between markers) -->
### Response tiers — inline attention icons

Tag lines within a response by attention level so you can calibrate scan-depth to task
complexity — skim tier 1, always read tier 4, dig into tiers 1 and 3 only when the work
warrants it. This is a different layer from the `✓`/`⚠` markers above (a simple FYI/done and
hard-gate marker) — all conventions coexist, none replaces another.

- 💭 self-talk / informational — routine narration, internal reasoning surfaced to you,
  standardized-process chatter. De-emphasized, skimmable — safe to skip without a second
  glance.
- ✨ about-to-act / did-this-for-you — an automated action completed inside a guided flow
  (sent a message, transitioned an issue, wrote a file, created a branch). Draws the eye.
- 💡 explanation — the meaningful why/what behind a decision or action, distinct from a plain
  about-to-act marker.
- ⚠ needs-you — a decision or answer is required, blocking. Same glyph as the hard-gate marker
  above; usable inline when a mid-response question needs an answer before continuing.

Never apply a tier icon decoratively — each is a deliberate signal. A line that doesn't fit
one of the four meanings gets no icon. Native CLI chrome (busy-spinner, recap lines) is not
governed by this convention.
<!-- end acp-response-tiers -->
